def: adj. & s.m. Free from want; possessing a competence or sufficiency; independent (of); able to dispense (with); rich, wealthy, opulent; — an independent person; a rich man.
s.f. Computation, calculation; enumeration; — estimate; judgment.
s.f. Computation, calculation; enumeration; — estimate; judgment.

ety: for A. غانٍ g̠āniṉ, act. part. of غني
def: adj. = g̠anī, q.v.
s.f. A harlot, courtezan; a dancing-girl; — a species of jasmine, Jasminum auriculatum.
s.m. Taker of spoil ( = A. g̠ānim), plunderer; enemy, foe, adversary.

ety: for A. غنيمة, inf. n. of غنم 'to get, or take, as spoil,' &c.
def: s.f. Plunder, spoil, booty; a prize; a boon, blessing, a God-send; a piece of good luck, good fortune; abundance; convenience; accommodation.

part. (f. -ā), Counted, numbered, enumerated, reckoned, calculated; — s.f. (but neut. in S.) Reckoning, enumeration-calculation, computation; number; — the science of computation, arithmetic; mathematics
subs: or gaṇit-kar, s.m. (f. -ī), An arithmetician; an astrologer
vidyā, vulg. gaṇit-bidyā, s.f. The science of calculation, mathematics.

def: s.m. (in Pers., Urdū, and Hindī), A store, hoard; treasure; hidden treasure; a treasury; a magazine, granary, store-house, grain-market; an emporium for grain; a place of merchandize, a market, mart; a case (of instruments, or brushes, or razors, &c.); — a heap, pile, stack, rick (of grain, or hay, or wood, &c.); — (in Hindī) a mine; jewel-mine; jewel-room; place where plate, &c. is preserved; — a tavern; — disrespect, contempt
subs: adj. Generous, liberal, lavish, profuse, extravagant
s.m. The burial-place of martyrs; a catacomb
s.m. One of the great treasures of Jamshed (it was dug up in the reign of Bahrām, and distributed amongst the poor)

def: s.m. Scald-head, baldness, Tinea capitis.
s.m. = gāṅjhā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀁𑀚 (gaṃja), from Sanskrit गञ्जा (gañjā).

def: adj. & s.m. = گنجا ganjā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Scald-headed; bald-headed, bald; — destroyed, effaced; — a scald-head, a bald-head.
s.m. Contempt; disrespectful treatment; contemptuous language; abuse.

ety: prob. for S. गन्धिनी; cf. gandhnī
def: s.f. A kind of aromatic grass, Andropogon schœnanthes (syn. gandh-bel, or gand-bel)
s.f. A kind of grass or verdure.
s.m. = ganjhail, q.v.
s.f. = ganjiyā, q.v.
s.m. = ganjhail, q.v.

s.m. The hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa; the leaves or young buds of the hemp-plant (the fructification, when nearly ripe, is bruised and smoked for intoxication: the dried leaves are ground in water, and drunk for the same purpose; in this state it is called bhaṅg, or sabzī)
subs: (or gāṅje-wālā), s.m. A vendor of gāṅjā or intoxicating preparations of hemp.

def: s.m.f. A person addicted to the use of gāṅjhā.
s.f. (Dakh.), corr. of kānjī, q.v.
s.m. = ganjīfa, q.v.
s.m. A pack of cards; — a game of cards
subs: s.m. A card-player; — a knave, trickster, cheat

def: s.m. A treasury; magazine, &c. (see ganj).

ety: ganj, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A wallet, a small bag; the coarse canvass bag of carters and grass-cutters.

ety: prob. fr. S. गञ्जं; P. گنج ganj, q.v.
def: v.t. To store, hoard, amass, heap up.

ety: prob. Prk. गज्जणअं; S. गर्जनीयं, rt. गर्ज्; or S. खर्जनीयं
def: v.t. To stir, agitate; to churn.
v.t. To treat with contempt; to treat disrespectfully; to abuse; — to destroy.
s.m.f. (dialec.) Addicted to smoking gāṅjhā; — an excessive smoker of gāṅjā.
s.m. A treasurer.
s.m. A mass of dough baked on embers; an inferior kind of bread made of arhar and other hard grains.

ety: gan = gannā, q.v. + kāṭā = kāṭ(nā)+Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. (f. -ī), The man employed to cut the sugar-cane into short pieces for the mill.

ety: gan = gannā, q.v. + kāṭā = kāṭ(nā)+Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. (f. -ī), The man employed to cut the sugar-cane into short pieces for the mill.
s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

def: v.t. See گننا ginnā.
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

def: s.m. A village, a hamlet
subs: s.m. Site of a village (whether in ruins or still standing)
or gāṅʼo-baṭ, s.f. Division of villages; division of a taʻalluqa into separate villages
or gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć, s.m. Village expenses or charges; municipal expenses
s.f. The cultivation, or the population, of a village
s.m. (f. -inī) = gāṅʼo-wālā, s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, a rustic.

def: v.n. & t. (Braj.) = gānā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. gaṇ.
s.f. The anus; the privities
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright

def: s.m. (rustic) = gāṅḍā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. corr. of gāṇḍīv, q.v.
s.m. A rustic corr. of ganḍāsā, q.v.
s.m. = gāṅḍū, q.v.
s.m. The angle or enclosed space at the junction of the fingers and of the toes.

ety: gāṅs˚, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. aff. इका
def: s.f. The iron head of an arrow or spear, &c.

ety: prob. Prk. गसणअं; S. ग्रस नीयं, rt. ग्रस्
def: v.t. To take in, inclose, hem in, surround, encompass; — to pierce, transfix, spit (as a fowl, &c.); to bore, perforate; to string, thread
s.f. = H گانٿنا गांटना gāṅṭnā, v.t. dialec. forms of gāṅṭh, and gāṅṭhnā respectively, qq.v.
s.m. (Dakh.), corr. of ghanṭā, q.v.
s.m. (Dakh.) = ganṭham, q.v.

s.f. A knot, tie, fastening; bond, engagement, stipulation; a knot or knob (in wood); a joint, articulation (as of a reed, or of the body, or of the muscles, &c.); a hardened or enlarged gland; a kernel; the navel; — a bundle, parcel, bale (of goods); a bunch; — a purse; a pocket; — entanglement, difficulty; misunderstanding; offence, displeasure; — dissimulation, guile
subs: or gāṅṭh bandhan karnā (-meṅ), To tie a knot (in, a handkerchief, &c., by way of a reminder); to tie together; to make an engagement or contract (with a person, for, -kī, a thing); to harbour prejudice, &c. (e.g. man-kī gāṅṭh bāṅdhnā)
adj. (f. -ī), Full of knots, knotty; intricate; — having full pockets, or having the pocket full of money
(-meṅ), To become knotted or entangled; (met.) misunderstanding or ill-feeling to arise; to harbour inveterate enmity (towards anyone, in the breast)
(-kī), To tie the knot (of), to splice; to tie the nuptial knot
To have a rupture at the navel
(-meṅ), To tie a knot (in, a string, or a handkerchief, for the purpose of reminding one of anything)
To be lost (a thing); to suffer loss
The navel to slip out of its place; to suffer from umbilical hernia
adj. (f. -ī), One's own purse, &c.; one's own; private
adj. & s.m. Having a full purse, wealthy; — a wealthy person
To waste or squander (one's) money; — to act to (one's) prejudice, to do oneself an injury, to incur loss
(-kī), To have (one's) purse cut off or stolen, to have (one's) pocket picked, to be robbed
v.t. To put in one's purse or pocket; to hoard up; — to pocket, to misappropriate

(-kī), To untie a knot or knots; to disentangle; — to open (one's) purse; to spend; — to remove prejudice, or misunderstanding, or ill-feeling; — s.m. The untying a knot; — opening the purse-strings, prodigality, extravagance; expense; — removal of prejudice, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Knotted; knotty (as a stick); compact (as a man)
v.t. To put in (one's) purse or pocket, to pocket

def: s.f. = gaṭh, q.v.
subs: s.m., and gaṅṭh-joṛā, s.m. See s.v. gaṭh.

def: s.m. A knot, &c. ( = gāṅṭh); — the knotted parts of the stalk and the ear-ends of straw separately piled on the threshing-floor (the 'colder' of English agriculture); grain mixed with straw.
s.m. = gaṭṭhā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) = gaṭhīlā, q.v.
s.m. A bricklayer; a builder.
s.m. An iron style (for writing on palm-leaves).
s.f. (dialec.) A knot, &c.
subs: s.m. An occupant of lands by heritable tenure.

def: s.f. A root or flake (of onion, &c.); a clove (see gaṭṭhā).
s.f. = gaṭhiyā, and gāṅṭh, qq.v.
v.t. To fasten or tie together, to thread, string; to arrange, connect in a regular series; to lay bricks (in mortar).

v.t. To fasten, tie, knot, string together, thread; to join, make adhere, put together, cobble, mend; to arrange, connect in a regular series; to fasten beads (or other ornaments) separately and at certain distances to the string that passes through and connects them all; — to string words together, to compose; to construct; to concoct; — to interlock; to clasp; — to impede, obstruct; — to win over, attach (to oneself); to reduce to obedience or subjection
A dialec. form of gaṭhnā, and gāṅṭhnā, qq.v.

ety: Prk. गंठडओ; S. ग्रन्थ+र+कः; see gaṭhṛī
def: s.m. (Dakh.) A capital stock, fund; bank (for money).
s.f. = gaṭhṛī, q.v.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
v.t. = H گانٿ गांट gāṅṭ, s.f. dialec. forms of gāṅṭh, and gāṅṭhnā respectively, qq.v.
s.m. A village, a hamlet
subs: s.m. Site of a village (whether in ruins or still standing)
or gāṅʼo-baṭ, s.f. Division of villages; division of a taʻalluqa into separate villages
or gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć, s.m. Village expenses or charges; municipal expenses
s.f. The cultivation, or the population, of a village
s.m. (f. -inī) = gāṅʼo-wālā, s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, a rustic.

def: s.m. contrac. of gāṅw, q.v.
= H گنوائي गंवाई gaṅwāʼī, = H گنوائين गंवाईं gaṅwāʼīṅ, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.
= H گنواهي गंवाही gaṅwāhī, = H گنوائين गंवाईं gaṅwāʼīṅ, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.
= H گنواهي गंवाही gaṅwāhī, = H گنوائي गंवाई gaṅwāʼī, s.f. corr. of gawāhī, q.v.

ety: gaṅwā˚ = Prk. गमावे = S. गम+आपि, caus. aug.
def: v.t. To lose; to lose or miss (a road, or one's way); to throw away, to get rid, of; to spend in vain; to waste, squander; to pass or spend (time); to trifle or fritter away (time, &c.)

ety: gāṅw, q.v.+ār = gen. suff. kar, q.v.
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, countryman, peasant, rustic; a clown, boor, churl; a low-bred person; — a stupid fellow, a dolt; — adj. Boorish, clownish; stupid ( = gaṅwārī)
subs: s.m. A villager's club; — (fig.) a blockhead, dolt, lout, fool.

def: s.m. A company or crowd of rustics.
adj. (dialec.) = gaṅwārī, q.v.

ety: gaṅwār, q.v.+ī = S. इकः, or ईयः
def: adj. Of or belonging to villagers or rustics; rustic, provincial; — clownish; stupid
subs: or gaṅwārī bolī, s.f. Rustic language or speech; provincial brogue.

ety: gaṅwār, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Rusticity; rudeness; clownishness; stupidity.
adj. & s.m. Rustic; rude, rough; clownish, boorish; — a rustic, &c. ( = gaṅwār).
s.m.f. Prodigal, wasteful; — a waster, squanderer, spendthrift.
adj. & s.m. = gaṅwī.

ety: gāṅw, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to a village or to the country, rural, rustic; — a rustic
subs: s.m. pl. Rustic hamlets or villages.

ety: prob. S. ग्रामिन्, = H گنويلا गंवेला gaṅwelā (f. -ī) [Prk. गामइल्लओ; S. ग्राम+इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. = gaṅwī.
part. adj. To be counted, admitting of calculation or computation, calculable; requiring counting.

ety: Pehl. go; Zend gāo; S. गो; Prk. गउओ, गउआ, गाओ
def: s.m. A bull, an ox, a bullock; — s.f. A cow
subs: s.m. A peculiar sleight by which an antagonist is thrown in wrestling (as an ox is thrown down by a butcher; cf. qasaiyā-dāv); — gāʼo-palang, s.m. A camelopard, a giraffe
s.m. A large pillow or bolster which supports the back of a person sitting
s.f. Grazing cattle; pasture-ground; — charge for grazing; — a tax levied on pasture-land
s.m. House or stall for cattle; cow-pen; cow-shed; — place where dead cattle are flayed
adj. Broken to pieces, ruined, destroyed
adj. Thick at one end and thin at the other; tapering, sloping; — s.f. A cow-tail; anything tapering; a cone; a pyramid; — a trumpet; a tube; — acclivity, descent
s.m. lit. 'Ox-eye'; a kind of bread of the shape of an ox-eye
s.f. A kind of bread of a long shape resembling neats' tongues; — the plant ox-tongue, bugloss
s.m. The bull on whose horns the earth is fabled to rest
or gāʼo-sar, s.m. The club or mace of Farīdūn
s.f. Enumeration (or census) of cattle; — a tax upon cattle
s.f. The (medicinal) gum of the opoponax tree; gum-resin
s.f. The sea-cow (from which the Persians suppose ambergris to come)
s.f. Killing a cow or an ox; slaughtering cows
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَاو (gāw). Doublet of گائے (gāe).

ety: gāʼo, q.v.+dī = Zend di = S. धी
def: adj. & s.m. 'Ox-witted,' stupid, ignorant, foolish; — a blockhead, booby, simpleton, fool.

ety: prob. by redupl. fr. S. ग्रभ्
def: s.m. Flattery, wheedling; deceit; artifice; fraud; embezzlement; — one who embezzles ( = next; — cf. ghāʼū-ghap)
s.m.f. Flattering; alluring; deceitful, fraudulent; — a flatterer, deceiver, humbug; — one who misappropriates or embezzles.
s.m. A cowherd.
s.m. = کانو gāṅw or gāṅʼo, q.v.
v.t. & n. (dialec.) = gānā, q.v.
s.m. Expense incurred in the municipal administration of a village; village charges (syn. gāṅʼo-ḵẖarć).
adj. Of or belonging to a village.

ety: prob. akin to P. guft, q.v.; cf. S. जल्पः
def: s.f. Prattle, talk, chat, tattle, gossip, idle talk, yarn, false report
subs: (or gapeṅ) mārnā (-kī), To prate (about), to tattle, to gossip, &c.
wety: Either onomatopoeic and inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑀧𑁆𑀧- (*gappa-), or inherited from Sanskrit गल्प (galpa). Cognate with Classical Persian گَپ (gap), Punjabi ਗੱਪ (gappa) / گپّ (gappa), Bengali গপ (gop), Gujarati ગપ (gap), Nepali गफ (gaph), Odia ଗପ (gapa), Assamese গপ (gop).

def: s.m. (local), The sound made by a blow, or in swallowing, &c.; — bragging, boasting, boast
s.m. & adv. The sound made in swallowing or gulping down anything; — gulp-gulp; with a gulping sound, or with repeated gulps; — abundantly, copiously
subs: v.t. To swallow with gulps, to gulp down.

def: v.t. To swallow with a noise, to gulp, to bolt; — to catch (as a ball, &c.).
s.f. (colloq.) = gap-shap, q.v.s.v. gap.
s.m. & adv. = gapagap, q.v. (cf. gab-gab).
adj. & s.m. Prating, tattling, addicted to gossip or to yarning, &c.; — prater, idle talker, tattler, babbler, gossip, yarner.
s.m. (local), The sound made by a blow, or in swallowing, &c.; — bragging, boasting, boast
s.m. A mouthful noisily swallowed; a bolting, or gulping; — (dialec.) a douceur or bribe swallowed; — a soft-sounding thrust or blow.
adj. & s.m. Prating, tattling, addicted to gossip or to yarning, &c.; — prater, idle talker, tattler, babbler, gossip, yarner.
s.m. (local), Boaster, bragger, swaggerer.
s.m. Hard soil of a whitish appearance.
see گپ شپ gap-shap.

ety: v.n. fr. غور 'to go down, sink,' &c.
def: s.m. A cave, cavern, pit, hole, den or lair (of a wild beast); a grotto; a hollow, low or depressed land or ground (where water lies); — a deep gash or wound
subs: (-meṅ), A hollow to form (in), to become hollow; a deep wound or sore to form.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَار (ġār), borrowed from Arabic غَار (ḡār). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi غار (ġar /⁠ġār⁠/).

def: s.f. = gārī, or gālī, q.v.
s.f. (Dakh.) Hail; — frost; — an agate.
A suffix denoting 'Maker, doer, agent' (e.g. ḵẖidmat-gār; parhez-gār; gunah-gār, &c., q.v.).
s.m. A drink, beverage, fluid; — a factitious poison; poison; — an antidote; — sickness, disease
s.m. (dialec.) = gal, q.v.
s.f. Itch in the throat and breast (of a horse); farcy.

ety: Zend kara; S. कर; cf. gār
def: s.m. Doer; maker; worker, workman (used as a suffix, e.g. zar-gar, 'a worker in gold, a goldsmith').
s.m. (dialec.) = gaṛh, q.v.
conj. If; — gar-ći, conj. Although.

ety: for A. غارة, n. of un. fr. غار
def: s.m. A cave, &c. ( = g̠ār, q.v.).
s.m. Hollow, hole, cavity, cavern; a ditch; low land (on which water does not lie long); — ambuscade
subs: To be concealed, to lie in ambush.

def: s.m. = گاڙا gāṛā, q.v.
s.m. = galā, q.v.
s.m. = garāʼo, q.v.
s.m. A large sheaf; a bundle of sheaves ( = garā, and garāʼo)
subs: s.f. Division of produce (or a crop) without threshing, by stacking the sheaves in proportionate shares.

def: s.m. Kneaded or prepared mud (see gārā); — a sort of coarse, thick cloth (see gāṛhā); — a sect of Moḥammadans forcibly converted from Hindūism in the reign of 'Alamgīr.
s.m. Thick mud; mud kneaded or prepared for making bricks, or pottery, or for building or plastering; — name of a rāginī or musical mode
subs: To prepare mud for plastering or building, &c.
To sing a gārā; (fig.) to tell a long story.

def: s.m. A common load-cart, a cart without a frame or box; a cart, waggon; gun-carriage.

ety: prob. gaṛ = gaṭ or ghaṭ = S. घटा+S. गच्छः, or गुच्छः
def: s.m. (dialec.) Thick close wood (or branches); a thicket; — denseness; crowdedness; — adv. Closely, densely; in a crowded or pressed manner.
s.m. Guard; watch, ward; — a guard-room.
adj. & s.m. Strange, wonderful; — strangers; — s.f. Strange or wonderful things or events.
s.f. pl. (of g̠arāʼib), Strange or wonderful things.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.) = garaj, q.v.
v.n. To thunder; to roar; to bellow; to crack with a report (as pearls, &c.)
wety: Adapted from Sanskrit गर्जति (gárjati). Doublet of گَاجْنَا (gājnā). Compare Maithili गरजब (garᵊjab) / 𑒑𑒩𑒖𑒥 (garajaba), Punjabi ਗਰਜਣਾ (garjaṇā) / گَرجݨا (garjṇā), Gujarati ગરજવું (garajvũ), Marathi गरजणे (garajṇe).

ety: garaj(nā) + wā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. A kind of vegetable (supposed to grow under the influence of thunder).
s.m. A kind of gold-fish, or gilt-head.
s.m. Poison, venom; venom of a snake.
adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous; — a venomous animai.

ety: for A. غرامة, inf. n. of غرم 'to pay, discharge,' &c.
def: s.f. A debt; a fine, mulct; tribute.
s.f. 'A place into which one sinks,' a swamp, morass.

ety: g̠ar˚ = orig. گر gar˚; rt. Zend gar = S. गर् (गिरति)
def: adj. Rapacious, fierce.
v.t. To cause to enter, cause to penetrate; to thrust (into), stick (into), drive (into), to poke or push (anything into); to pierce, prick; to put into a hole (in the ground), to bury
s.m. (rustic) An enclosure of mud round a mill, &c.
s.f. Boasting, boast, vaunt, brag (syn. baṛang)
subs: or gaṛang hāṅknā, To boast, brag.

def: s.m. A boaster, a braggart.
s.m. A boaster, a braggart.

ety: prob. Prk. गड्डंतं, imperf. part. of गड्डणअं = gaṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat; — a ceremony performed to remove the influence of an evil spirit (an effigy of the demon is made of dough and painted with red lead (sendūr), and to this offerings are made; it is then shown to the sick person or passed over him from head to foot, after which the effigy is burnt and the charm is completed); — a mode of incantation for the destruction of any person (māraṇ), or to secure his affection (mohan), or to subject him to obedience (basīkaran), or to imprison him or deprive him of the power of action or speech (stambhan), or to drive him away (uććāṭan), or to call him (ākarshaṇ; — certain words are pronounced over an effigy of the person (or his name in writing) which is after wards buried).

ety: prob. Prk. गड्डंतं, imperf. part. of गड्डणअं = gaṛnā, q.v.
def: s.m. The game of tip-cat; — a ceremony performed to remove the influence of an evil spirit (an effigy of the demon is made of dough and painted with red lead (sendūr), and to this offerings are made; it is then shown to the sick person or passed over him from head to foot, after which the effigy is burnt and the charm is completed); — a mode of incantation for the destruction of any person (māraṇ), or to secure his affection (mohan), or to subject him to obedience (basīkaran), or to imprison him or deprive him of the power of action or speech (stambhan), or to drive him away (uććāṭan), or to call him (ākarshaṇ; — certain words are pronounced over an effigy of the person (or his name in writing) which is after wards buried).
s.m. Neck-rope (for securing an animal); a tether.
s.m. A coarse kind of salt.
s.m. A burying.
s.m. A stock or collection of sheaves; — a chopper (to cut fodder with; — syn. gaṅḍāsā).
s.m. An instrument for cutting jawāṛ stalks, &c. for fodder; a kind of chopper ( = garā, or garāʼo).

ety: prob. fr. S. गर, rt. गॄ+gap; see gap-gap, and gapaknā
def: adj. Taken down, swallowed up, lost; — s.m. A gulp
subs: or gaṛap-denī, adv. In the giving of a gulp, in a trice, suddenly; — down you go, in you go, down with you, &c. (e.g. gaṛap thailī-meṅ, 'down with you (or down you go) into the bag').

def: adj. corr. of g̠arq, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = garuḍ, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. of A. غرغرة; or A. غرارة, inf. n. of غرّ
def: s.m. Gargling; a gargle.
s.m. A sack for holding the walls (qanāt) of a tent; (loc. or dialec.) an enclosing or encircling wall or fence; a line of investment, &c.

ety: prob. S. ग्रह+कारिका; cf. ghirnī or ghernī
def: s.f. An instrument for twisting thread or string, a reel; the pulley or block over which a well-rope passes; (loc. or dialec.) a groove, a rut; the groove or rut in which moves the wheel of a mill for preparing mortar, &c.
s.m. = gaḍariyā, gaṛeriyā, qq.v.
s.m. A snake-charmer, a juggler, conjurer.
s.m. = gāraṛu, q.v.
s.m. = gāraṛu, q.v.
s.f. = gaṇḍāsī, q.v.
s.f. = gaṇḍāsī, q.v.

ety: for A. غارة, fr. اغارة ig̠ārat, 'making a raid,' &c., inf. n. of اغار, iv of غور 'to come to the low land or country,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) 'A raid, foray'; plunder, pillage, rapine, havoc, devastation
subs: v.n. To be plundered, be spoiled, be marred, be destroyed or ruined

s.m. Sudden loss or destruction; — adj. Suddenly or unexpectedly lost; — g̠ārat-g̠ol honā or ho-jānā, To be suddenly or unexpectedly lost; to be swept away, to be ruined or destroyed, to be annihilated; to disappear
v.t. To plunder, spoil, ravage, lay waste, ruin, destroy
s.f. The act of plundering, or of laying waste, &c.; pillage; destruction

def: s.m. Light soil, poor soil.

ety: inf. n. of غرض 'to be vexed or disquieted' (by
def: s.f. An object of aim or pursuit, or of desire, or of want; aim, end, object, design, view, purpose, intention; business; meaning; a want, need, necessity, occasion; interest, concern; interestedness, interested motive; spite, hatred, rancour; — adv. In short, in a word, in fine
subs: or g̠araẓ-āmez, adj. Designing; interested, selfish; spiteful, malevolent
or g̠araẓ-kā bāʼolā, s.m. A slave to (one's) desires or passions
(-kī), To feel the need (of); to have occasion (for)
(-kī), To have desire (for), to aim (at); to be interested (in); to busy or concern ooneself (with)
adj. Desirous (of), wishing (for); needing, needy; concerned, interested; selfish, designing
(apnī), To attain (one's) object, gain (one's) end, accomplish (one's) desire or purpose
adj. & adv. Disinterested, having no concern (with), having no purpose to serve; — disinterestedly; indifferently
wety: First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi غَرَض (ġrẓ), borrowed from Classical Persian غَرَض (ġaraz), from Arabic غَرَض (ḡaraḍ).

ety: g̠araẓ, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. From interested motives, interestedly, selfishly; spitefully, malevolently.
adj. Interested, self-seeking, selfish, having some end to serve, designing, artful.

ety: inf. n. of غرب 'to retire from view,' 'to disappear,' &c.
def: s.m. Setting (of the sun, or a star); — the west (syn. mag̠rib).
s.m. 1˚ = garbh, q.v.; — 2˚ = garv, q.v.

ety: gaṛ˚ = gaḍ = gaṭ = ghaṭ = S. घटा; baṛ is prob. a jingle; cf. gaḍ-baḍ, gaḍ-maḍ, and gaṭ-paṭ
def: s.m. Disorder, derangement, irregular intermixture, jumble, muddle; confusion, commotion, tumult, disturbance; clamour, uproar, hubbub; hurry, bustle, stir, pother; mental disturbance, distraction, bewilderment; rugged or interrupted state of ground, uneven or broken ground; — adj. In confusion, in a muddle, jumbled, mixed up; higgledy-piggledy, topsy-turvy; miscellaneous, motley, promiscuous; — rough, rugged, uneven (ground)
subs: s.m. Foul weather; disorder; disturbance, &c. ( = gaṛbaṛ)
v.t. To confuse, confound, jumble, mix up; to make a mess of, &c.
v.n. To be confused, to be mixed up, to be jumbled; to be in a muddle; to be distracted, or alarmed, &c.

def: s.m. Confusion; bustle; agitation, alarm, &c. ( = gaṛbaṛ); — rumbling (of the bowels), borborygmi.
s.f. Confusion, commotion, disturbance, tumult, bustle; flutter, agitation; scare, fright, alarm ( = gaṛbaṛ; cf. ghabrāhaṭ).
v.n. To be confused, to be confounded; to be disturbed (in mind); to be alarmed; — to rumble (the bowels; — in this sense also guṛbuṛānā).
s.f. (Dakh.) = gaṛbaṛāhaṭ.
s.f. = garvatā, q.v.

s.m. Womb; belly; inside, middle, interior (of anything); — fœtus, embryo; — conception, pregnancy; — the fruit (of plants); a sprout; — the bed of a river (especially of the Ganges) when fullest, i.e. on the fourteenth day of the dark half of the month Bhādoṅ, or in the height of the rains (as high as the river flows at this period, so far extends the garbh; after which the tīr or proper bank begins and extends for one hundred and fifty cubits; this space being holy ground)
subs: (˚bha+ādh˚), s.m. Impregnation; conception; — a ceremony performed after menstruation to ensure or facilitate conception; — a ceremony performed on the first indications of pregnancy
s.m. The hair of a new-born child
or garbh-pātan, s.m. Miscarriage (after the fourth month of pregnancy), abortion; — garbh-pāt karnā, To cause or produce abortion
adj. Causing miscarriage; — s.m. A red kind of Moringa (supposed to produce abortion)
s.m. 'A slave by birth'; the son of a slave-girl
or garbh-rūpī, s.m. A child, an infant; a youth (cf. gabrū)
(-ko), To become pregnant, to conceive; — garbh-se honā, v.n. To be pregnant
s.m. The abode of the fœtus, the womb
To dwell in the womb (said of an avatār, q.v.)
or garbh-vantī, adj. & s.f. Pregnant, with child; — a pregnant woman

def: adj. Impregnated (with); pregnant, with child.
adj. & s.f. Pregnant; teeming; — a pregnant woman; — the plant Asclepias rosea.
adj. Western, occidental.
adj. = garvit, q.v.
conj. Although (see gar).

ety: rt. of gardīdan; Pehl. vart˚; Zend vareṭ; S. वर्त् (वृत्)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Going round, revolving; traversing, travelling or wandering over, or through, or in (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jahān-gard, 'One who has travelled over or around the world); — s.f. Dust; — the globe; — fortune
subs: s.f. A tax formerly levied on travellers
(-kī), Dust to fly about; (fig.) to be or become ruined or desolate (a city, &c.)
or gard-ālūda, adj. Covered (or soiled) with dust, dusty; — encumbered with riches or worldly cares
v.t. To make dusty, to dirty (clothes, &c.); to make dim, to dim, overshadow, cast a gloom over; to eclipse
v.n. To be or become dust
to be worthless or good-for-nothing; to be as dust
to be easily removed or overcome, to be practicable or easy (e.g. měḥnat-ke āge sab gard hai).

def: s.f. = gārad, q.v.
s.m. corr. of gard, 'dust,' q.v.
s.m. Dust ( = gard); a kind of powder.
s.m. An ass ( = gadhā); — the white lotus.
s.f. A she-ass ( = gadhī); — a kind of beetle generated (and living) in cow-dung, a dung-beetle.
s.f. The neck
subs: or gardan kāṭnā, or gardan mārnā (-kī), To behead, decapitate
s.m. A chain, collar, necklace, ornament for the neck
(-kā of the blood, and -kī or -rī of the person), To have (one's) blood on the neck (or head), to be guilty of the blood (of)
(kisī-kī), 'To mount the neck (of),' to tyrannize (over), to practise violence, or extortion, &c. (upon)
(apnī), To put the neck in a noose; to involve oneself in difficulty or trouble; to incur responsibility
To bow the neck; to submit, yield
(-kī), To be beheaded; — to be ruined or undone
adj. & s.m. 'Exalting the neck'; proud, haughty, vain; insolent, refractory, rebellious, disobedient; stubborn, obstinate; — a proud person, &c.
s.f. Pride, arrogance, presumption, insolence; rebelliousness, disobedience; stubbornness; — gardan-kashī karnā, To be proud or arrogant; to rebel, &c.
(-kī), To twist (or to wring) the neck (of); to strangle, to throttle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْدَن (gardan). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi گردن (grdn /⁠gardan⁠/).

ety: rt. of gardānīdan, caus. of gardīdan; and = gard, q.v.+suff. ān
def: part. Causing to revolve or go round, revolving, turning; winding; changing; inverting; converting (used as last member of compounds); — s.f. Turning, going round, revolving, revolution; winding; a swimming (of the head); inflexion, declension; conjugation; revision; — adj. Trained (e.g. gardān kabūtar)
s.m. The neck ( = gardan)
subs: a blow on the nape of the neck
(-ko), To give (one) a blow on the nape of the neck.

def: s.f. The pointers (two stars in the Great Bear).
s.f. Turning, revolving, &c. (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. A covering for the neck (of a horse), a horse), a horse-cloth; — a blow on the nape of the neck; — a trick in wrestling; — a silver ring worn on the neck; — a cornice
subs: (-ko), To drive (a man) by the neck
(-ko), To strike (one) on the nape of the neck.

def: v.t. To turn, turn over; to inflect; to conjugate; to wrap up, roll up; to close, shut (as a book, &c.); to go over, repeat, revise (a lesson); — to regard, esteem.
s.m.(?) Name of a tribe of Saiyids in the district of Muzaffarpūr.
s.f. Wandering, travelling; — change; revolution; — separation; downfall, misfortune, affliction (used as last member of compounds)
subs: s.f. A calamity on the nobility; downfall of the aristocracy
s.f. Dethronement of a king; revolution; anarchy
s.f. The Paṭhāṇ invasion (under Aḥmad Shāh Durrānī).

ety: perf. part of gardīdan; see gard
def: part. Turned; turned away; converted; changed; estranged.
s.f. Going round, turning round, revolution; circulation; roll; course; period; turn, change; vicissitude; reversion; — adverse fortune, adversity; — wandering about, vagrancy
subs: To go or turn round, to revolve; to circulate (as the blood); to wander about
To be or become in a state of revolution, &c.; to fall into adversity, to become unfortunate.

ety: S. गर्त+oṛā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) A small pit.

ety: gard, q.v.+ūn = wan = wān = Zend vant = S. वन्त् (nom. वान्)
def: s.m. A wheel; the heavens, the firmament, the celestial globe or sphere; chance, fortune (and her revolving wheel); — an engine (for pulling up trees by the roots, &c.), a windlass; — a chariot, chaise; a child's go-cart (by which he learns to walk).
s.m. Land mortgaged for a time, or until the loan taken is repaid by the rents.
s.m. Circuit; round; — adv. Around, round about (e.g. jahāz ke garer dauṛ, 'running round the ship').
s.m. (dialec.) The receptacle for the sugar-cane before it is cut at the mill.
s.f. A joint or piece of the sugar-cane (the portion between two knots).
s.m. A shepherd, goat-herd.

ety: rt. Zend gared, fr. gar = S. गर् (गॄ)
def: s.m. Noise, clamour, exclamation, shout, uproar, tumult; a mob; — lamentation, complaint, groaning; growling; gurgling noise in the throat.
s.m. Name of one of the ten principal Munis; — Name of a sect of Brāhmaṇs (descended from Garg)
subs: or garg-purohit, or garg-r̤ishi, or garg-muni, s.m. = garg, q.v.
vulg. garg-bansī, s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts.

def: s.f. = gauraiyā, q.v.
s.m. A scaffold; — a castle, a tower; a bastion; — a cavalier.
s.m. = گرگج gargaj, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) Thick, dense, crowded, full, densely packed; miscellaneous, motley.
s.f. (Dakh.) Overflowing copiousness or plenty; great press or thronging; — a medley, mash; miscellany.
s.m. A whirlpool, an eddy; — a churn.
s.f. Gurgling, rumbling; the gurgling sound made by the water in a ḥuqqa.

ety: for A. غرغرة, inf. n. of غرغر 'to gargle'
def: s.m. Gargling; making a bubbling noise (as water in boiling, &c.); rattling sound in the throat; the death-rattle.
s.m. A common kind of ḥuqqa; a large smoking-pipe.
s.f. = gaṛgaṛ, q.v.

ety: gargarā˚ = S. गर्गर+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment
def: v.n. To gurgle; to rumble; to roll, to thunder; to roar (as a tiger, &c.).
v.n. = gargarānā, q.v.
s.f. = gaṛgaṛāhaṭ = gaṛgaṛ, q.v.
s.f. A gugglet, a water-vessel.
s.m. A kind of grass which grows in low grounds during the rains.
s.m. Old tattered clothes, tatters, rags.
s.f. A difficulty; a misfortune.
s.m. A fort; citadel; castle; — a wheeling hovel, or engine of war, under cover of which soldiers approach a fort
subs: To take a fort; (fig.) to surmount a difficulty
s.f. Moat or trench of a fort; an entrenchment.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Thick (as cloth, &c., or a fluid mixture of any kind), close, dense, gross; muddy, turbid; close, intimate (friendship); deep (sleep, colour, &c.); stout, substantial; strong (infusion, &c.); stiff, difficult, arduous; sturdy, compact; bold, undaunted, valiant (in fight); sly, shrewd, knowing, wise, proficient, adept, smart; — s.m. A kind of coarse, thick cloth.
s.f. Censure, blame, reproach, abuse.
s.m. A hole, hollow, cavity, pit; a grave; a ravine; an abyss
subs: or gaṛhā bharnā, To fill up a hole or hollow; — to make up a deficiency; — to take (one's) fill
To lie concealed in a pit or hole, to lie in ambush.

def: s.f. Burying.

ety: gaṛhā, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A pit, a large hole; a small pond or tank.
s.f. Making, make, workmanship; malleating (jewellery); — the price of malleating jewellery, or of graving, &c.
s.m. A burier.
s.m. Censuring, blaming; censure; blame, reproach.

ety: caus. of gaṛhnā; — gaṛhā˚ = gaṛhāw˚ = Prk. गढ़ावे = घड़ावे = S. घट+आपय˚; rt. घट्
def: v.t. To cause to make, form, or fashion (especially by beating or hammering); to form, fashion; to malleate; to grave.
v.t. To bury, &c.

ety: gāṛhā, q.v. + pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Thickness, closeness, denseness, &c. (see gāṛhā).
= S گارهستهيه गार्हस्थ्य gārhasthya (fr. gr̤iha - stha), Of or relating to the condition of a householder; fit for, or incumbent on, a householder.
= H گارهستهك गार्हस्थिक gārhasthik (fr. gr̤iha - stha), Of or relating to the condition of a householder; fit for, or incumbent on, a householder.

ety: gaṛh(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (i.e. āpi, caus. augment + aff. ti)
def: s.f. Make, form, fashion, shape, mould, build; workmanship ( = gha ṛat).
s.f. A dialec. form of gaṛhat, q.v.
v.t. = gaṛhānā.
s.m. A hole, pit, cavity; a ditch; an abyss.
s.f. A small fort; a castle; a village fortification of mud flanked with towers (under former governments there was scarcely a village in India without its gaṛhī)
subs: s.m. A description of muʻāfī tenure, in Bundelkhand, by which lands are held on paying a stipulated yearly tribute (but not one-fifth the amount which ought to be paid)
To take a fort, &c. ( = gaṛh jītnā, q.v.).

def: s.m. = gaṛhelā, q.v.

ety: gaṛh˚, rt. of gaṛhnā = gaṛnā+iyā = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इका
def: s.f. A pike, a short javelin, a small spear; a goad.

ety: gaṛhā, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A pit, a large hole; a small pond or tank.
s.m. Name of a town and celebrated place of Hindū pilgrimage situated on the Ganges to the east of Meraṭ (a fair is held there in the month of Māgh).
v.t. (dialec.) = gāṛnā, q.v.

ety: gaṛh˚ = Prk. गढ(इ) = S. घट(ते), rt. घट्
def: v.t. To make, form, fashion, shape; to forge; to form by hammering, to malleate; — to mould, cast; — to practise the goldsmith's or the founder's art (cf. ghaṛnā); — to smooth, plane, rasp; — v.n. To be made or fashioned (esp. by beating or hammering), to be formed; to be graved; to be moulded; to be cast, &c.
v.t. To malleate, to form by hammering, &c.
v.n. = gaṛnā, q.v.

r: gaṛhwār, gaṛhwāṛ, gaṛh, gāṛha, wār, wālā
def: adj. Coarse, thick (cloth, &c.); gross, fat.

r: gaṛhwāṛ, gaṛhwār, gaṛh, gāṛha, wār, wālā
def: adj. Coarse, thick (cloth, &c.); gross, fat.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Deserving reproach, contemptible, mean, low, vile; — a contemptible or mean man.
s.f. 1˚ = gālī, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of gāṛī, q.v.
s.f. A cart; car, carriage, coach; (for rel-gāṛī) railway-carriage, railway-train; — gāṛī-bhar, adj. & s.m. A cart-load of; a cart-load; — wide enough for a cart (to pass; e.g. gāṛī-bhar rastā)
subs: To harness a horse or horses (or to yoke bullocks) to a conveyance; — to let carts, &c. on hire
To drive a cart, &c.; — to hire out carriages
s.m. A carriage-house, coach-house; — a cab-stand
A cart to be robbed; — carriage (of a train) to be cut off
or gāṛī-wān, s.m. A carter, carman; carriage-driver, coachman

def: s.m. (dialec.) A friend, an intimate companion.
s.f. (dialec.) = gaṛhī, q.v.
s.f. Acting, doing; practice; trade, office (used as last member of compounds, as zar-garī; sitam-garī, qq.v.).
(see gār), suff. fem. Doing, making, &c. ( = garī; e.g. ḵẖidmat-gārī).
s.f. Prick, thrust, stab; entering (into), piercing, penetrating.
s.f. Kernel (of any nut or seed, as the cocoanut, walnut, &c.); pulp (of fruit, as the jack).
adj. & s.m. corr. of غريب garīb, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. غرب 'to become distant, to go far away,' &c.; see g̠urbat
def: adj. & s.m. Foreign, alien; strange, wonderful; rare, unusual, extraordinary; — poor, destitute; meek, mild, humble, lowly; — a stranger, foreigner, an alien; — a poor man; a meek or humble person
subs: adj. & s.m. In a foreign land; — a foreigner, stranger
s.f. Cherishing of, or kindness to, the poor
s.m. The humble dwelling (of your servant), my house
or g̠arīb-kī mār, s.f. Oppression of the poor; misfortune
adj. & s.m. Courteous to strangers; kind to the poor, hospitable; — one who is kind to the poor ( = g̠arīb-parwar)
s.f. Courtesy or attention to strangers, hospitality; kindness to the poor ( = g̠arīb-parwarī).

ety: for A. غريبة, fem. of g̠arīb
def: adj. & s.f. = g̠arīb, q.v.

ety: g̠arīb, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Fit for the poor; humble; — like a stranger; like a poor person; humbly.

ety: g̠arīb, q.v.+ī, Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Foreignness, strangeness; — poverty, indigence, wretchedness; meekness, mildness, lowliness, humility.
adj. & s.m. Drowning; drowned; immersed, submerged; overwhelmed; — a drowning person
subs: adj. Overwhelmed with mercy, drowned in the grace of God.

def: s.m. Agaric.
s.f. Heaviness, weight, gravity; importance, dignity, venerableness; worth, excellence; self-importance, vanity; boast.

ety: corr. of, or by mistake for, A. عرين ʻarīn
def: s.m. A forest, wood, thicket; covert, haunt, place of resort (of the lion, tiger, &c.).
s.m. (old H.), corr. of grīshma, q.v.
adj. Heaviest; excessively heavy; very venerable; worst.
s.m. A low hill, a hillock; a high mound or bank; an acclivity; a ravine; a steep or difficult pass.
s.m. = galiyā, q.v. (and cf. galiyar).
adj. Perverse, stubborn, obstinate, stiff-necked; stout-hearted.
s.f. Stoutheartedness; — stubbornness, obstinacy.

ety: prob. contrac. fr. A. غريزة 'nature,' &c.; rt. غرز 'to plant'
def: s.m. Meekness, gentleness, mildness, clemency, forbearance.

ety: rel. n. fr. g̠arīzat, 'nature'; rt. غرز 'to plant,' &c.
def: adj. Natural, native, innate.
adv. With a roar, &c.
ex: listen to the roar of the lion!

def: s.m. (and f. in Urdū), Sound, noise; a grumbling noise, a growl; roaring, roar; bellowing; rumbling (of clouds, &c.), thundering; thunder; — passion, wrath
subs: s.m. A thundering, storming; bellowing and roaring

ety: contrac. of garjāhaṭ, v.n. fr. garajnā, q.v.
def: s.f. (dialec.) Thundering, thunder; sound of thunder, &c.
s.f.(?) (dialec.) = garjāṭ, and garjan, qq.v.
s.m. The muttering of clouds, the sound of distant thunder.

ety: v.n. fr. غرق 'to drown, to sink'
def: s.m. Drowning, sinking, immersion; — adj. Drowned, immersed, sunk, overwhelmed; absorbed, engrossed, deep (in)
subs: s.m. Water deep enough to drown, deep water; a whirlpool
(-meṅ), To drown; to be immersed (m), be sunk or steeped (in); to be deep (in), be absorbed (by), be engrossed (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَرْق (ġarq), from Arabic غَرْق (ḡarq).

def: adj. Submerged, overflowed, inundated; — s.f. Flooding land; flood, inundation; flooded land; low ground
subs: To be flooded; to lie low; to be lowered or depressed (ground).

def: adj. Light-blue, grey (in colour, like the eyes of a cat).
s.m. (dialec.) = gaḍḍā, q.v.
s.m. Rock or fossil salt (especially that found in the district of Sānbhar, in the province of Ajmīr).

ety: Pehl. garm; Zend garema; S. घर्म
def: adj. Hot, warm; in a state of heat; burning; glowing: fervid; ardent, zealous, fervent; excited; eager, intent on; fiery, choleric, virulent; active, lively, brisk (as a market, &c.)
subs: adj. Hot and hot, piping hot, all hot; — new, fresh; — spontaneous, unpremeditated, extempore
and garm-ṣǒḥbat, s.f. Warmth of friendship, strong attachment, close companionship, intimacy
s.f. Activity or briskness in a market, brisk (or great) demand, good sale; high value or estimation (of anything)
To speak warmly or angrily; to speak readily and to the purpose
s.f. Warmth of affection, cordiality; ebullition; empressement, ardour, zeal, enthusiasm
adj. & s.m. Going hastily, pressing on hotly; — a lover travelling in haste to his beloved
adj. Speaking with warmth; speaking forcibly or effectively
or garm-o-sard, adj. & s.m. Hot and cold; lukewarm; — ups and downs (of life); prosperity and adversity; accidents or vicissitudes (of time or fortune)
adj. & adv. Hastening; in hot haste
v.t. To warm, heat; to make angry, to excite, incense; to make eager or active, to stimulate, spur, incite; to exercise
vulg. garam masālā, s.m. Spices, seasoning
v.n. To be or become hot; to become heated or excited; to be or become angry; to be busy, or active, or brisk, to be thronged (a market, &c.); to be rife.

def: s.m. (uninflec.) Heat, warmth, sultriness; the hot season, summer
s.m. A hot (or tepid) bath.

ety: garmā(nā) + āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Becoming heated or hot; heat, warmth (cf. garmāʼī).
s.f. Becoming heated or hot; heat; excitement; — a stimulant
subs: (-ko), To become heated; to be hot (a thing); to be or become excited or inflamed (with passion or lust).

ety: garm, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To become warm or heated or hot ( = garm honā); to be or become angry; to become enlivened

ety: garm, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; warm or hot weather, the hot season; warmth, glow; fervour, fervency, ardour; activity, briskness, throng (of a market); — heat of temperament; heat of system, morbid heat; fieriness, vehemence; passion, rage, anger, excitement; attachment, warm affection; sexual passion, lust; heat; — the venereal disease
subs: v.n. To be hot (the weather; e.g. aj baṛī garmī paṛtī hai, 'it is very hot to-day')
Excess or predominance of heat (of the weather, or of the temperament or system); a discharge of bloody urine (a disease in elephants, horses, &c.)
(-meṅ), To produce heat or warmth (in); to become angry (with, -se); to make a show of affection, to affect fondness (for)
s.m. pl. Clothes for the warm weather, summer clothing
(apnī), To satisfy sexual passion or lust; to indulge in carnal intercourse
(-ko), To suffer from heat of the system; — to have the venereal disease
s.f. Cold and heat; luke-warmness (in affection)

ety: gār˚ = S. गालय(ति), caus. of rt. गल्
def: v.t. To strain, filter; — to squeeze, press; to milk.

ety: gāṛ˚, prob. Prk. गाढ(इ), fr. S. गाढ, p.p.p. of rt. गाह्; cf. gāṛhnā and gaṛhnā
def: v.t. To drive (in or into), drive down, to sink, to imbed (in), to fix firmly; to unite; to bury; to cover (a fire) with ashes; to set, to plant
v.n. A dialec. form of gaṭhnā, q.v.

v.n. To penetrate (into, -meṅ), to enter (into), to stick (into), to pierce; to be driven (into), to be firmly fixed (in); to sink (in or into); to be put (into the ground), to be buried; — s.m. A mark, sign, trace
